ref: God looked at what he had done. All of it was very good! Evening came, then morning—that was the sixth day.
text: Eye Mawu kpo nusiwo katã wòwɔ la, eye kpɔ ɖa, wonye ŋutɔ. Ale zã do, eye ŋu ke, eya nye ŋkeke adelia.
translated: The Lord is near to the brokenhearted and saves the crushed in spirit. The Lord is near to the brokenhearted; he saves the crushed in spirit, a righteous man may have many troubles.

ref: So Isaac named the well Shibah, and the town is still called Beersheba. Esau's Foreign Wives
text: Eye wòna ŋkɔe be Siba, eyata du la ƒe ŋkɔ nye Beer-Seba vaseɖe egbegbe. Esau ɖe srɔ̃.
translated: And he called it Siba, well of the oath, because of the oath between them, at Beer-sheba, until today. And Esau despised his birthright.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Eye ne lã sesẽtɔwo le asi yɔm la, Yakob tsoa ati ŋɔŋlɔawo dana ɖe lãwo ƒe ŋku me le tsinonu la mè, bena woafɔ fu ɖe ati ŋɔŋlɔawo ŋu.
translated: And it came to pass, when men began to multiply on the face of the earth, and daughters were born unto them, that the sons of God saw the daughters of men that they were fair;

ref: Abraham gave Abimelech some sheep and cattle, then the two men made a peace treaty.
text: Tete Abraham tsɔ alẽwo kple nyiwo na Abimelex, eye wo kple eve wòbia nu.
translated: And Abraham sojourned in Gerar, and the Philistines stopped up all the wells

ref: The Lord God took some soil from the ground and made a man. God breathed life into the man, and the man started breathing.
text: Azã Yehowa Mawu tsɔ anyi mè ame, eye wògbɔ agbegbɔgbɔ ɖe eƒe ŋɔti me; ale ame la zu luʋɔ gbagbe.
translated: The Lord is my shepherd, I lack nothing, he makes me lie down in green pastures; surely his goodness and love will follow me
